title: SabioTrade Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026)

meta_description: SabioTrade Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026). SabioTrade (3.8★, $119/mo). Step-by-step setup, features walkthrough, and pro tips. Updated 2026.
excerpt: Complete guide to setting up and using SabioTrade — from account creation to pro-level tips.
content:
What is SabioTrade?

SabioTrade is a proprietary trading firm founded in 2023 that lets traders access funded accounts up to $650,000 through a simplified 1-step evaluation process—no multi-phase challenge required. The platform is built by CODEVIL IT ENGINEERING LIMITED and operates from Dublin, Ireland. With a 3.8/5 rating on Trustpilot, it appeals to beginners and experienced forex traders who want quick approval, weekly payouts (typically within 24 hours), and industry-leading 90% profit splits on larger accounts. SabioTrade supports forex, stocks, indices, commodities, crypto, and ETFs, making it versatile for different trading styles.

How to Create Your SabioTrade Account

Getting started with SabioTrade takes roughly 15–20 minutes. Here's the process:

  • Visit the signup page and enter your email address. You'll receive a confirmation link immediately.
  • Complete your profile with your first and last name, country of residence, and phone number. SabioTrade verifies that you're 18+ and in an approved jurisdiction.
  • Choose your evaluation tier and assessment fee. The Essential plan starts at $119/month, but you can also opt for higher tiers if you want immediate access to larger funded account sizes.
  • Verify your identity using a government-issued ID (passport or driver's license) and proof of address. This typically takes 1–3 hours, though can extend to 24 hours during peak periods.
  • Fund your assessment fee via credit card, debit card, or cryptocurrency. SabioTrade refunds this fee in full after your first profit payout, so the cost is recoverable.
  • Receive your live account credentials once verification clears. You'll get your MetaTrader 4 or MetaTrader 5 login details and can start trading immediately.

Unlike firms requiring a 2-phase challenge (thinking of FTMO or similar competitors), SabioTrade's 1-step evaluation means you're trading real capital within 24 hours of approval—a massive advantage for traders who want to start fast.

Setting Up SabioTrade for the First Time

Once your account is approved, log into your SabioTrade dashboard. The interface is designed for simplicity, not complexity.

Your main dashboard shows: Account balance, current profit/loss, withdrawal status, and your trading rules summary. Unlike cluttered platforms, SabioTrade strips away noise so you can focus on trading metrics that matter.

Key settings to configure immediately:

  • Risk Management Thresholds – Set your daily loss limit and maximum drawdown tolerance. SabioTrade enforces hard stops at these limits to protect your funded account. The default settings are conservative (daily loss limit 5% of account), but you can adjust them based on your risk profile.
  • Payout Preferences – Decide how often you want profit withdrawals. SabioTrade processes payouts weekly (typically arriving within 24 hours to your bank account or crypto wallet). Set a minimum withdrawal amount if you want to batch profits.
  • Broker Connection – SabioTrade provides MT4 and MT5 access directly. Enter your provided credentials into your trading platform and confirm the connection. No additional broker setup is needed.
  • Notification Settings – Enable alerts for important events: daily loss limit warnings, withdrawal approvals, account status changes, and news feed updates. Most traders recommend push notifications so you don't miss critical account events.
  • Two-Factor Authentication – Enable 2FA on your dashboard login. This is non-negotiable with live funded capital at stake.

The entire setup takes 5 minutes. SabioTrade deliberately avoids overwhelming new traders with configuration options—you can start trading within an hour of account approval.

Essential Features You Should Know

1. Mobile App – SabioTrade's mobile app lets you monitor positions, check balances, and manage withdrawals from anywhere. If you're trading forex or indices across time zones, the mobile app is critical for staying responsive to market moves. Real-time notifications alert you when you're approaching daily loss limits or when a payout is approved.

2. Performance Analytics – The analytics dashboard breaks down your trading statistics: win rate, average win/loss, profit factor, Sharpe ratio, and monthly profit curves. Unlike generic trading platforms, SabioTrade aggregates this data specifically for your funded account, helping you track whether you're consistently profitable over time. Use this to identify losing months and adjust your strategy.

3. Risk Management – SabioTrade's built-in risk management enforces hard stops: daily loss limits (typically 5%), maximum account drawdown (typically 10%), and daily trade limits. Once you hit these thresholds, trading stops automatically. This protects both you and the firm. You can't override these limits—they exist to prevent account blowouts.

4. News Feed – Integrated economic calendar and news updates appear directly in your dashboard. This is particularly useful for forex traders (SabioTrade's core audience) who need to know about interest rate decisions, inflation data, and central bank announcements. You won't miss critical economic events that move your traded pairs.

5. Education & Mentorship – SabioTrade includes free education resources: webinars, trading guides, and one-on-one mentorship calls with experienced traders. This is rare in the prop trading space, where most firms charge separately for mentorship. Use this to strengthen your edge—especially if you're a beginner.

6. Weekly Payout System – Profits are calculated every Friday and paid to your bank account or crypto wallet within 24 hours. There's no waiting 30 days or going through dispute resolution. SabioTrade's speed here is a competitive advantage over firms with monthly payouts.

7. Scaling Rules – Be aware of a limitation: SabioTrade doesn't offer scaling plans to grow your funded account size over time. Other firms (like comparison firms) let you unlock bigger accounts after hitting profit targets. SabioTrade keeps your account size fixed. This matters if you outgrow your initial $100K–$650K allocation.

SabioTrade Pricing: Which Plan Should You Choose?

SabioTrade's pricing structure is straightforward: you pay a monthly subscription and an upfront assessment fee, both refunded after your first profit payout.

  • Essential – $119/month – Funded account up to $100K. For beginner forex traders testing their strategy with real capital. This is the lowest barrier to entry. Profit split: 85%.
  • Plus – $289/month – Funded account up to $250K. For traders ready to scale beyond the starter tier but not yet handling six figures. Profit split: 90%.
  • Advanced – $479/month – Funded account up to $400K. Designed for consistently profitable traders. Profit split: 90%.
  • Ultimate – $939/month – Funded account up to $550K. For serious traders managing mid-six-figure accounts. Profit split: 90%.
  • Prime – $2,989/month – Funded account up to $650K (the maximum). For institutional-level traders managing large portfolios. Profit split: 90%.

Which plan should you choose?

If you're a beginner or trading part-time, start with Essential ($119/month). The $100K account is enough to test your edge without excessive risk. Once you're consistently profitable for 3–6 months, move to Plus ($289/month).

If you're already a professional trader with a track record, jump directly to Advanced or Ultimate ($479–$939/month). These tiers unlock the 90% profit split immediately, maximizing your earnings. The monthly fee is negligible compared to your trading profits at these account sizes.

Prime ($2,989/month) is overkill for most traders. Only consider it if you have a proven strategy with massive monthly profits and need the $650K capital ceiling.

Critical note: SabioTrade refunds your assessment fee after your first payout, so the actual cost is the monthly subscription. If you make $500 in your first month (entirely realistic), your assessment fee is recovered. After that, you're only paying the subscription for access to funded capital.

Pro Tips for Getting the Most Out of SabioTrade

1. Use the 1-step evaluation to your advantage – Unlike FTMO's 2-phase challenge, SabioTrade's single evaluation means you can start trading live immediately. Get your account approved, trade for 2–3 weeks in real market conditions, and collect data on your win rate and profit factor before scaling. This real capital test is more valuable than any demo backtesting.

2. Exploit the 90% profit split on accounts $100K+ – If you're making $50–$100/day in profits, that 90% split (vs. 85% on the Essential tier) translates to thousands of dollars annually. The jump from Essential to Plus ($170/month difference) is worth it the moment your monthly profits exceed $150–$200. Calculate your ROI: If Plus generates an extra $600/year in profit splits, the extra $2,040/year in fees is a wash—move up.

3. Monitor your risk management limits religiously – SabioTrade's daily loss limit (typically 5%) stops you out automatically. Don't treat this as a soft guideline. Experienced traders hit their loss limit and stop for the day, avoiding revenge trading. Use the first half of each week for profitable trades and bank early gains; reserve Fridays for higher-risk setups only if you're well ahead of your daily limit.

4. Request your payout on Thursday night, before Friday settlement – SabioTrade calculates profits every Friday and processes payouts within 24 hours. If you submit a withdrawal request Thursday evening, your funds often arrive by Saturday morning, giving you weekend access to your profits. This matters if you're reinvesting profits elsewhere.

5. Leverage the free mentorship for your first 30 days – SabioTrade's free mentorship is included but often overlooked. Use your first 30 days to schedule 2–3 calls with experienced traders on their team. Ask specific questions about forex volatility (if that's your niche), risk management optimization, and how to grow your account size post-scale. This is $500+ of value at other firms.

6. Track the economic calendar religiously – SabioTrade's integrated news feed highlights economic releases. Set alerts for high-impact events (FOMC decisions, NFP, ECB announcements). Adjust your trading before these events, as they often trigger 100+ pip moves in forex pairs. Many SabioTrade traders skip this step and blow accounts on surprise rate hikes.

7. Export your performance analytics monthly – Download your performance data every month and analyze it outside the platform. Calculate your Sharpe ratio, win rate, average risk-reward ratio, and monthly profit targets. This forces accountability and prevents you from drifting into undisciplined trading habits.

Common SabioTrade Issues and How to Fix Them

Issue 1: Payout delays or disputes – Some traders report that SabioTrade's customer support is slow to resolve payout issues, particularly when a withdrawal is flagged for compliance review. Fix: Submit all withdrawal requests with clear documentation of your trades (screenshots of your profit report from the dashboard). If a payout is delayed beyond 48 hours, escalate to their support team via email and request a status update. Document everything. Most disputes are resolved within 5 business days if you provide evidence of your profitable trading.

Issue 2: Daily loss limit hit too early – New traders sometimes hit the 5% daily loss limit in the first hour of trading, eliminating their entire trading day. Fix: Reduce your position size. If your daily loss limit is $5,000 (on a $100K account) but you're trading 1 micro-lot per trade, you're risking too much per position. Scale back to 0.1 micro-lot position sizes until you're consistently profitable over a full month. Let your win rate prove itself before increasing size.

Issue 3: No account scaling available – After hitting $10,000+ in monthly profits, traders realize SabioTrade doesn't offer a scaling plan to grow your funded account beyond $650K. Fix: This is a fundamental limitation, not a bug. If you've maxed out SabioTrade's account size and are consistently profitable, graduate to a traditional prop firm (like FTMO or Topstep) that offers scaling, or manage your own capital through a retail broker.

Issue 4: Futures trading not supported – SabioTrade restricts you to forex, stocks, indices, commodities, crypto, and ETFs. Futures contracts (ES, NQ, GC) are off-limits. Fix: If futures are central to your trading strategy, SabioTrade isn't the right fit. Consider alternative firms that support futures contracts.

Is SabioTrade Worth It? Our Verdict

SabioTrade is worth it if you're a beginner or intermediate trader seeking fast approval, simple evaluation, and weekly payouts. The 1-step evaluation cuts your time-to-funded-account from 2–3 months (with other firms) to 24 hours. The 90% profit split on accounts above $100K and free education justify the monthly subscription. However, SabioTrade is not for traders who want scaling plans, futures access, or accounts beyond $650K. If you're already managing serious capital or need leverage and complex instruments, look elsewhere. For everyone else—especially forex traders and prop trading newcomers—SabioTrade delivers real value at reasonable pricing. Read our full SabioTrade review for detailed performance data and trader testimonials.
author: TradingToolsHub Editorial
